pr.title: Rename ftKb_SpecialNFx to ftkirbyspecialfox, match most functions
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Renamed Kirby's copied Fox/Falco blaster file from ftKb_SpecialNFx.c to ftkirbyspecialfox.c and expanded the file from mostly placeholders/partial matches into a much more complete decompilation. The PR matched many Kirby Fox/Falco SpecialN functions, added descriptive names for two previously anonymous symbols, updated build/split/symbol references, and reused enums and helper inlines to handle Fox vs Falco motion-state and blaster-item differences.

key_files.role: New renamed implementation file; replaces ftKb_SpecialNFx.c and contains the added matches for Kirby's copied Fox/Falco blaster SpecialN behavior.
Deleted old file name; previous partial implementation with placeholders and unnamed functions.
Updated split entry from melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNFx.c to melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialfox.c for text/sdata2 range 0x800FDC70-0x800FEDD0.
Renamed fn_800FDCE0 to ftKb_SpecialNFx_OnChangeAction and fn_800FE0E0 to ftKb_SpecialNFx_CreateBlasterShot.
Updated object list to compile the renamed ftkirbyspecialfox.c file.
Updated prototypes for the newly named OnChangeAction and CreateBlasterShot helpers.
Minor type/header adjustment noted in changed files; exact diff not visible in excerpt.

what_changed: Renamed the source unit from ftKb_SpecialNFx.c to ftkirbyspecialfox.c in source, splits.txt, and configure.py.
Added a 713-line implementation file replacing the deleted 372-line partial file.
Matched ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDC70, which computes a hand/joint-relative blaster position using FtPart_R3rdNa and lb_8000B1CC, falling back to a zero vector on null inputs.
Renamed fn_800FDCE0 to ftKb_SpecialNFx_OnChangeAction and kept its behavior of calling ft_800892A0 and ft_80089824 with FORCE_PAD_STACK_8.
Renamed fn_800FE0E0 to ftKb_SpecialNFx_CreateBlasterShot, a small wrapper around ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30.
Replaced literal motion-state IDs in ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDD4C and related code with ftKb_MS_Fx/FcSpecialN* and ftCo_MS_Throw* enum names where available.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDDF4 to check whether Kirby is in relevant Fox/Falco copied blaster states or throw states and return based on fp->x2070.x2071_b6.
Added inline helpers to select Fox vs Falco blaster item IDs and ground/air start, loop, and end motion IDs based on fp->fv.kb.hat.kind.

Implemented or matched ground and air start/loop/end animation handlers for copied Fox/Falco blaster, including item calls, motion-state transitions, accessory4_cb assignment, and loop/end branching via fp->mv.fx.SpecialN.isBlasterLoop.

Kept small IASA, physics, and collision functions simple: B-button loop flag setting for start/loop IASA, ft_80084F3C for ground physics, ft_80084DB0 for air physics, ft_80083F88 for ground collision, and ftCo_AirCatchHit_Coll for air collision.

Bot report showed 36 new matches and +1592 matched bytes overall, with notable matches including ftKb_FxSpecialAirNLoop_Anim, ftKb_FxSpecialNLoop_Anim, ftKb_FxSpecialAirNEnd_Anim, ftKb_FxSpecialNStart_Anim, ftKb_FxSpecialAirNStart_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDDF4, and many tiny IASA/Phys/Coll wrappers.
Bot report also listed apparent broken matches for the old ftKb_SpecialNFx unit, which are attributable to the file/unit rename rather than necessarily functional regressions.
smart_moves: Performed the filename rename consistently across source, splits, symbols, header prototypes, and configure.py so the object and symbol database remained coherent.

Converted anonymous symbols to role-based names once their behavior was clear: OnChangeAction for the callback-style ft_800892A0/ft_80089824 routine and CreateBlasterShot for the ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 wrapper.
Used small inline selector helpers for Fox-vs-Falco differences instead of duplicating large switch blocks throughout every action function.
Preserved matching-sensitive artifacts such as FORCE_PAD_STACK_8, PAD_STACK(8), volatile/new_var temporaries, and temporary assignment patterns around Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
Reused known motion-state enum names after the enum issue noted in the old code was apparently resolved, making the decomp easier to search and less magic-number-heavy.

Modeled item lifecycle and callback behavior explicitly: created the blaster item with it_802AE8A8, stored it in fp->fv.kb.xB0, set death/take-damage callbacks, scheduled accessory4_cb to CreateBlasterShot, and cleared or removed the item through existing item functions.
Kept Fox and Falco behavior in one copied-blaster file while selecting data attributes and motion IDs by hat kind, which fits Kirby copy-ability organization.
decomp_lessons: When a file represents a copied character-specific ability, a descriptive lowercase module name such as ftkirbyspecialfox.c can be clearer than an abbreviation-heavy legacy name, but all split/build references must be updated together.
Anonymous fn_ symbols should be renamed only when behavior gives enough evidence; here, callback names were supported by usage sites and function bodies.

For matching fighter action code, tiny wrapper/callback functions can matter: even a 0x20 wrapper like CreateBlasterShot should be preserved and named rather than inlined away if the original binary has it.
Inline helper functions can reduce repeated Fox/Falco switch logic while still matching, provided the compiler emits the expected code at call sites.
Motion-state enum adoption is useful once enum values are stable; it improves readability without relying on comments beside raw numeric cases.
Callbacks such as death2_cb, take_dmg_cb, accessory4_cb, and x21EC are central to matching special-move item behavior; future work should trace both item calls and fighter callback assignment.
Decomp.dev match reports around file renames can show old-unit broken matches and new-unit matches simultaneously; interpret them as a relocation/rename effect before assuming a regression.
Stack padding, volatile locals, redundant temporaries, and assignment-inside-call patterns remain important matching tactics for this codebase.
naming_conventions: The PR kept existing exported prefix style for functions: ftKb_SpecialNFx_* for shared copied Fox/Falco blaster helpers and ftKb_FxSpecial* for Fox-copy action callbacks.

New local inline helpers used descriptive names such as ftKbGetBlasterId, ftKbGetStartMotionId, ftKbGetAirStartMotionId, ftKbGetLoopMotionId, ftKbGetEndMotionId, ftKbGetAirLoopMotionId, and ftKbGetAirEndMotionId.
Motion states use ftKb_MS_FxSpecial* for copied Fox and ftKb_MS_FcSpecial* for copied Falco, with common throw cases using ftCo_MS_ThrowB, ftCo_MS_ThrowHi, and ftCo_MS_ThrowLw.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used FORCE_PAD_STACK_8 in ftKb_SpecialNFx_OnChangeAction and PAD_STACK in routines where stack shape mattered.
Preserved matching-shaped locals such as volatile int pad, unsigned long long pad, temporary HSD_GObj* variables, and redundant Fighter* aliases in animation end routines.
Used assignment inside Fighter_ChangeMotionState call arguments in loop handlers, e.g. temp = gobj, matching original codegen patterns.
Used helper inlines for motion-state selection so source stays readable while still allowing expected inlined switch codegen.
Kept null checks around gobj and Fighter* in simple query/cleanup functions to match defensive original control flow.
For start actions, reset cmd_vars individually, zeroed velocities explicitly, and assigned fp->mv.fx.SpecialN.isBlasterLoop = false before creating the item.
Used OSReport("ftToSpecialNFox::Caution!!!\n") plus HSD_ASSERT line-number-specific assertions for failed blaster creation paths.
Used item API calls in action-frame order: it_802ADDD0 status/update calls, it_802AE538/it_802AE608 for command-variable-triggered item behavior, and it_802AEAB4 for item removal/cleanup.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 matched code increased to 55.24%, +0.04%, +1592 bytes.
Reported 36 new matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialfox, including large animation routines and many small IASA/Phys/Coll wrappers.
Reported 3 improvements in unmatched items: ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE100 to 98.62%, ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE240 to 98.75%, and .sdata2 to 57.14%.

Reported 30 broken matches in the old ftKb_SpecialNFx unit and a .sdata2 regression there; because this PR renamed the unit, this evidence is best interpreted as old-name/new-name accounting unless corroborated by functional review.
No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs touching ftkirbyspecialfox.c to see whether ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE100 and ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE240 reached 100% from their 98% states.
Search for subsequent renames of ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDC70, ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDD4C, ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDDF4, and ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 to learn final semantic names.
Check whether types.h changes clarified fp->mv.fx.SpecialN.isBlasterLoop or Kirby copied blaster struct layout.
Compare with Fox/Falco native SpecialN implementations to reuse item lifecycle and motion-state selection patterns.
Look for PRs addressing decomp-dev old-unit broken match reporting after file renames.
